topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Best RSP-Consensus alignment available at 1.05 — Consensus #8 Tier 2 (CLE) and RSP #5 DoT 86.3 Starter grade. As a high-RSP-affinity GM I weight Waldman's top-5 placement heavily, and Concepcion's flanker/slot versatility plus return-game upside is exactly the Tier-2 anchor I need with the top 4 (Love/Tate/Tyson/Lemon) off the board.

topTargets.name: Kenyon Sadiq
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #5 Tier 2 (TE1, NYJ) — only Tier 2 player whose drop past 1.05 would be a steal. My roster is critically thin at TE (1 rostered) and Brock Bowers high-end comp from RSP is enticing despite RSP only ranking him #30. Consensus tier wins this tiebreak; landing spot bakes in NFL fit RSP's pre-draft list missed.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: Prime 2.05 target — Consensus #21 Tier 3 (PHI) but RSP #11 DoT 84.4 'on cusp of Starter tier.' Classic high-affinity RSP value gap (10 spots) at a position of dire roster need. Move TE in Philly's offense fits my contention timeline given 5 contracts expiring.

topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: Massive RSP-Consensus arbitrage if he falls to 2.05 — RSP #4 DoT 87.2 Starter grade with Nico Collins comp, but Consensus only #22 Tier 3 (NYG). My 40% RSP weighting screams here even though I'm WR-deep (10 WRs); Waldman top-5 talent at a Round 2 price is the kind of swing a high-affinity GM cannot pass.

topTargets.name: Jadarian Price
topTargets.reasoning: Backup 1.05 target if Concepcion goes earlier — Consensus #7 Tier 2 (RB2, SEA) and RSP #21 DoT 83.5 with 'ceiling as high as RBs 2-5.' RB room is only 4 deep with contracts turning over, and Seattle landing spot post-draft elevates him above where pure RSP scouting placed him.

capPosture: Workable cap at $34.7M used / $10.3M space — 1.05 rookie can go active roster, but 2.05 and 3.05 likely route through taxi (50% hit) to preserve flexibility for the 5 expiring contracts.

wildcard.name: Skyler Bell
wildcard.reasoning: Quintessential high-RSP-affinity 3.05 reach — RSP #6 DoT 85.9 Starter grade with Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp, but Consensus has him in the Free Agents tier (#33) and MFL ADP doesn't crack the top 30. As a Waldman devotee with 38% A/B-tier RSP players already rostered, this is exactly the lottery ticket I bet on while ADP-driven GMs ignore him.

summary: Maverick is a high-RSP-affinity contention-window team (5 contracts expiring) with a glaring TE hole and a workable but not generous cap. The plan: anchor 1.05 on the best RSP-Consensus overlap (Concepcion or Sadiq for positional need), exploit Waldman value gaps at 2.05 (Stowers or Fields), and swing for a Bell-style RSP sleeper at 3.05 to taxi. Active-roster the first-rounder, taxi the rest to keep cap room for the post-expiration roster reshape.
